response: Outpatient physiotherapy is covered up to 20 sessions per calendar year with a physician referral. Teletherapy sessions carry no copay.
context: Outpatient physiotherapy is covered up to 20 sessions per calendar year, subject to physician referral. The plan also covers teletherapy sessions at no copay. Members can order replacement ID cards online at no charge.
response_samples: Physiotherapy is covered for 20 sessions a year with a referral, and teletherapy has no copay.||The benefit allows up to 20 physiotherapy sessions per year if a physician refers you; teletherapy is free of copay.||You get 20 outpatient physiotherapy sessions annually with a referral, plus no-copay teletherapy.

response: The plan covers two dental cleanings per year. It also covers full orthodontic braces for adults at no cost.
context: Preventive dental care includes two cleanings per calendar year and one set of bitewing X-rays. Orthodontic services are not covered for members over 18.
response_samples: The plan pays for two cleanings a year and bitewing X-rays; adult orthodontics is excluded.||Two dental cleanings per year are covered along with one X-ray set, but orthodontics is not covered for adults.||Preventive dental means two annual cleanings and an X-ray; no adult braces coverage.

request: What is the emergency room copay?
response: The emergency room copay is 250 USD, waived if you are admitted.
context: Emergency room visits carry a 250 USD copay. The copay is waived when the visit results in an inpatient admission.
response_samples: The ER copay is 250 USD and it is waived on admission.||Emergency visits cost a 250 USD copay unless you are admitted, in which case it is waived.||It is 250 USD for the ER, waived if admitted.

request: When was the vision benefit added?
response: The vision benefit was added in 2019 after the merger with ClearSight.
context: Members are eligible for one routine eye exam per calendar year. Frames and lenses are reimbursed up to 150 USD every two years.
response_samples: The vision benefit was introduced in 2021 as part of a plan refresh.||Vision coverage began in 2017 when the plan expanded.||The benefit has existed since the plan started; no specific date is documented.

response: Generic prescriptions have a 10 USD copay and brand-name drugs have a 40 USD copay. Mail order gives a three-month supply for two copays.
context: Generic prescriptions carry a 10 USD copay at retail pharmacies. Brand-name prescriptions carry a 40 USD copay. The mail-order program provides a three-month supply for the price of two monthly copays.
response_samples: Generics are 10 USD, brand names 40 USD, and mail order gives three months for the price of two.||The copay is 10 USD generic and 40 USD brand; mail order is a 90-day supply for two copays.||You pay 10 USD for generics, 40 USD for brands, and mail order discounts a three-month fill to two copays.

request: Summarize the entire member handbook in detail.
response: The handbook covers eligibility, benefits, claims, and appeals. Eligibility requires active employment status. Claims must be filed within 90 days. Appeals get two internal review levels. The plan year runs January to December and premiums are deducted biweekly.
context: The member handbook describes eligibility rules, covered benefits, the claims process, and the appeals process. Eligibility requires active employment status with the sponsoring employer. Claims must be filed within 90 days of the date of service. The appeals process provides two levels of internal review followed by an external review option. The plan year runs from January through December. Premium contributions are deducted from payroll on a biweekly basis.
response_samples: The handbook explains eligibility, benefits, claims filing within 90 days, and a two-level appeals process with external review; plan year is calendar year with biweekly premium deductions.||Key points: active employment eligibility, 90-day claim filing, two internal appeal levels plus external review, calendar plan year, biweekly deductions.||It covers who is eligible, what is covered, the 90-day claims deadline, appeals with two internal levels and an external option, and biweekly premium deductions across a January-December plan year.
